diluted earnings per share (EPS) attributable to Disney decreased to $1.27 compared to $1.81 in the prior-year quarter

validation.rule: Each metric must appear in ≥2 distinct locations OR have one high-confidence source (income statement). Material conflicts MUST be flagged.
validation.detail: All four metrics pass validation. Revenue and EPS·diluted are confirmed by two agreeing sources each. Net income requires careful interpretation: total consolidated net income ($2,465M) and Disney-attributable net income ($2,247M) are distinct line items (not a conflict), and the attributable figure is corroborated by both the income statement and MD&A. EPS·basic appears in a single high-confidence income statement source. No material conflicts detected.

per_metric.reason: Two distinct sources agree exactly: income statement (page 18, raw 25168.0) and MD&A Consolidated Results (page 42, '$25.2 billion'). Rounded MD&A figure is consistent within 0.1% of the precise income statement value. High confidence.

The $2,465M figure is total consolidated net income (including noncontrolling interests) — a separate, valid line item. Disney-attributable net income of $2,247M is confirmed by both the income statement (page 18) and MD&A (page 42, '$2.2 billion'), agreeing within 1%. No conflict; the two net income figures represent different scopes. High confidence on the attributable figure.

Two GAAP sources agree exactly at $1.27: income statement (page 18) and MD&A Consolidated Results (page 42). Both are explicitly labeled GAAP. No non-GAAP figure present, so no GAAP vs. non-GAAP comparison needed. High confidence.

Single source: income statement (page 18, GAAP). No second location found. Source is a high-confidence primary financial statement, but the one-source rule caps confidence at med absent a second corroborating location.

narrative: No prior-quarter or prior-year data was provided, so relative comparisons cannot be made. Revenue was $25.2B, net income was $2.2B, and diluted EPS was $1.27 for the current quarter.

confidence.summary: The filing itself is clean — GAAP-only EPS (no gap), validation passed, and 7 corroborations across 5 distinct sources push confidence up. The dominant drag is the single available period in the EPS series (no historical continuity to verify) and effectively no usable price-trend data (only 2 data points, 0 coverage days), leaving cross-source and price-alignment factors heavily down-weighted.

factors.name: Inter-document agreement
factors.signal: Clean GAAP-only EPS (eps_gap: null), validation passed, no conflicts
factors.detail: eps_gap is null, meaning the filing reports GAAP-only EPS — the cleanest possible case. Validation passed with zero conflicts flagged. 7 corroborations were found across 5 distinct sources for 4 metrics, with a conf_tally of 2 high / 2 med / 0 low. No red flags anywhere in the intra-document picture.

factors.name: Cross-source consistency
factors.signal: Only 1 period available; EPS = $1.27, 0 sign reversals, 0 low-conf periods
factors.detail: Only 1 period (10-Q) is available in the EPS series ([1.27]), so there is no historical continuity to assess. While the single data point shows no sign reversals and zero periods with low-confidence metrics, a single observation cannot confirm trend stability or cross-period coherence. This fundamentally limits the weight this factor can carry.

factors.name: Insider activity & news
factors.signal: Net insider buying of ~$9M over 90 days; headlines corroborate strong operational momentum
factors.detail: Over the 90-day insider window: 10 buys vs. 9 sells, with a net buy value of $8,997,141.52 — a meaningful net positive despite near-balanced transaction count, suggesting larger buy sizes. Recent headlines are broadly supportive: Toy Story 5 debuted with the biggest opening weekend of 2026 ($160M), franchise records, and a Shanghai resort expansion. No headlines contradict or raise doubts about reported financials.

factors.name: Price-trend alignment
factors.signal: Price data severely limited: 2 data points, 0 coverage days, no 30d/90d change available
factors.detail: Price coverage is critically thin — only 2 data points over 0 coverage days, with pct_change_30d and pct_change_90d both null and aligns_with_price null. The price_slope_sign is +1 (positive direction) and earnings_direction is 0 (neutral/flat), which is neither confirmatory nor contradictory, but the data is far too sparse to draw any meaningful alignment conclusion. This factor is heavily down-weighted to 0.10 as a result.
